message:
fix(ffs-extent): reject extent mutation ranges past 32-bit logical block space (bd-xh0ia)

A fresh-eyes audit of the extent-mutation surface found that
punch_hole was forming hole_end via saturating_add over u64, while
the same logical-range computation in map/mark paths goes through
the checked_logical_range_end helper. Two bugs followed:

  * punch_hole and collapse_range silently accepted (logical_start,
    count) tuples whose end exceeded the ext4 32-bit logical block
    space. saturating_add clamped the end to u64::MAX, then the
    overlap test still walked the tree and either hit a no-op or
    inadvertently consumed extents that had nothing to do with the
    caller-intended window.
  * insert_range never validated the input range at all, even though
    the same precondition applies — its phase-1 split path delegates
    to punch_hole on a zero-length window only after callers crossed
    the boundary check.

This commit threads the existing checked_logical_range_end helper
into all three mutation paths:

  * punch_hole now binds hole_end via the helper and propagates the
    resulting FfsError::InvalidGeometry.
  * collapse_range hoists the cut computation in front of the
    phase-1 punch and sources it from the helper instead of an
    unchecked u64 add (which previously could overflow before
    phase-2 used it as a >= comparator).
  * insert_range gains an explicit precondition call before the
    extent-split phase. The result is intentionally discarded — the
    helper's role here is fail-fast validation, not a new return
    value, since insert_range computes its own per-extent shifted
    ranges later.

Three new regression tests (one per public entry point) drive each
function with logical_start = u32::MAX, count = 2, and assert:

  * the call returns Err(FfsError::InvalidGeometry(_));
  * the formatted message names both the rejected operation and the
    "exceeds ext4 32-bit block space" phrase that the helper emits.

A small assert_invalid_logical_range helper centralizes the matcher
so all three tests stay in lockstep with the helper's error contract.

The .beads/issues.jsonl entry for bd-xh0ia is opened to in_progress
in this commit; closure follows once full ffs-harness conformance
re-runs cleanly. The bd-elah2 entry is reordered into chronological
position and bd-4bit6's status/closed_at/close_reason fields are
re-canonicalized by the bead writer — pure formatting, no semantic
delta on those two beads.
